dataset: House of the Dragon — StoryAtlas first dataset

validation.result: 0 errors, 0 warnings — all location_id / characters_involved / event_ids references resolve within each episode; all episode entity ids exist in the shared registries; all relationship endpoints exist in the character registry.
validation.unused_note: Canonical entities kept in the registry for consistency and future episodes; not an error.

season_3.covered: s03e01 (enriched: added Aegon's flight from King's Landing, the Dragonstone launch of Jacaerys & Baela, Addam & Alyn at the Gullet, and Jason Lannister's death — 7 events / 7 scenes)
season_3.not_covered: s03e02 — aired 2026-06-28 but no reliable plot source was available at generation time (title officially unannounced; reported writer Sara Hess, director Clare Kilner). Left out deliberately rather than fabricated.

sources.title: Wikipedia — House of the Dragon season 1
sources.used_for: S1 episode titles, air dates, plots

sources.title: Wikipedia — House of the Dragon season 2
sources.used_for: S2 episode titles, air dates, plots

sources.title: Wikipedia — House of the Dragon season 3
sources.used_for: S3 coverage, S3E1 plot, air dates

sources.title: A Wiki of Ice and Fire — Greens
Sky / Den of Geek — Greens vs Blacks guides

open_gaps: s03e02 plot not yet documented (see coverage.season_3).
Location coordinates (x/y) are all null — to be placed manually once a base map is chosen.
A few canonical registry entities are not yet referenced by any episode (see validation.unused_*).

westeros_base_map.reason: No clearly free/CC-licensed Westeros map was confirmed at generation time. Per task rules, official HBO maps must not be copied blindly.
westeros_base_map.how_to_add: Place a chosen map image at data/hotd/assets/westeros-map.<ext>, prefer a freely/Creative-Commons or fan-licensed map, and record its source + license in data/hotd/assets/README.md. Then set the image and its pixel dimensions in the app's Map view and place locations (coordinates currently null).

conventions.id_scheme: stable kebab-case slugs (e.g. "kings-landing", "rhaenyra-targaryen"); shared across episodes for dedup/consistency.
conventions.registry_files: characters.json / locations.json carry a small wrapper (kind/series/note); episode files follow the strict episode data contract.
conventions.summaries: factual, written in our own words; no verbatim wiki/transcript passages.

data_usage_estimate: Well under the ~2 GB budget. Only text pages were fetched (a handful of Wikipedia season pages) plus a few web searches; no large media assets were downloaded — on the order of a few megabytes total.
